Our innovative contribution includes the translation of various sentence similarity datasets into Arabic, enabling a comprehensive evaluation framework to compare these models across different dimensions. We trained several nested embedding models on the Arabic Natural Language Inference triplet dataset and assessed their performance using multiple evaluation metrics, including Pearson and Spearman correlations for cosine similarity, Manhattan distance, Euclidean distance, and dot product similarity. The results demonstrate the superior performance of the Matryoshka embedding models, particularly in capturing semantic nuances unique to the Arabic language. Results demonstrated that Arabic Matryoshka embedding models have superior performance in capturing semantic nuances unique to the Arabic language, significantly outperforming traditional models by up to 20-25% across various similarity metrics. These results underscore the effectiveness of language-specific training and highlight the potential of Matryoshka models in enhancing semantic textual similarity tasks for Arabic NLP.
摘要：该工作提出了一种通过Matryoshka嵌入学习来训练阿拉伯语嵌套嵌入模型的新框架，该框架利用多语言、特定于阿拉伯语和基于英语的模型，以突出嵌套嵌入模型在各种阿拉伯语NLP下游任务中的能力。我们的创新贡献包括将各种句子相似性数据集翻译成阿拉伯语，使一个全面的评估框架能够从不同的维度比较这些模型。我们在阿拉伯自然语言推理三元组数据集上训练了几个嵌套嵌入模型，并使用多种评估指标来评估它们的性能，包括余弦相似性、曼哈顿距离、欧几里德距离和点积相似性的Pearson和Spearman相关性。结果表明Matryoshka嵌入模型具有优越的性能，特别是在捕捉阿拉伯语特有的语义细微差别方面。结果表明，阿拉伯语Matryoshka嵌入模型在捕捉阿拉伯语言特有的语义细微差别方面具有优越的性能，在各种相似性度量上显著优于传统模型20-25%。这些结果突显了特定语言训练的有效性，并突显了Matryoshka模型在提高阿拉伯语NLP语义文本相似性任务方面的潜力。

[NLP-37] Entropy Thermodynamics and the Geometrization of the Language Model
[NLP-37] 热力学与语言模型的几何化

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21092
作者: Wenzhe Yang
关键词-EN: language models, language, discuss how pure, pure mathematics, mathematics and theoretical
关键词-ZN: 语言模型，语言，讨论如何纯粹、纯粹的数学，数学和理论
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Statistical Mechanics (cond-mat.stat-mech); High Energy Physics - Theory (hep-th); Differential Geometry (math.DG)
备注: 18 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In this paper, we discuss how pure mathematics and theoretical physics can be applied to the study of language models. Using set theory and analysis, we formulate mathematically rigorous definitions of language models, and introduce the concept of the moduli space of distributions for a language model. We formulate a generalized distributional hypothesis using functional analysis and topology. We define the entropy function associated with a language model and show how it allows us to understand many interesting phenomena in languages. We argue that the zero points of the entropy function and the points where the entropy is close to 0 are the key obstacles for an LLM to approximate an intelligent language model, which explains why good LLMs need billions of parameters. Using the entropy function, we formulate a conjecture about AGI. Then, we show how thermodynamics gives us an immediate interpretation to language models. In particular we will define the concepts of partition function, internal energy and free energy for a language model, which offer insights into how language models work. Based on these results, we introduce a general concept of the geometrization of language models and define what is called the Boltzmann manifold. While the current LLMs are the special cases of the Boltzmann manifold. Comments: 18 pages Subjects: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Statistical Mechanics (cond-mat.stat-mech); High Energy Physics - Theory (hep-th); Differential Geometry (math.DG) MSC classes: 68T01 ACMclasses: I.2.7 Cite as: arXiv:2407.21092 [cs.CL] (or arXiv:2407.21092v1 [cs.CL] for this version)
摘要：在本文中，我们讨论了如何将纯数学和理论物理应用于语言模型研究。利用集合论和分析方法，给出了语言模型的严格数学定义，并引入了语言模型的分布模空间的概念。我们利用泛函分析和拓扑学建立了广义分布假设。我们定义了与语言模型相关的熵函数，并展示了它如何允许我们理解语言中的许多有趣现象。我们认为，熵函数的零点和熵接近于0的点是LLM逼近智能语言模型的关键障碍，这解释了为什么好的LLM需要数十亿个参数。利用熵函数，我们提出了关于AGI的一个猜想。然后，我们展示了热力学如何为我们提供对语言模型的直接解释。特别是，我们将定义语言模型的配分函数、内能和自由能的概念，这些概念提供了对语言模型如何工作的见解。基于这些结果，我们引入了语言模型几何化的一般概念，并定义了所谓的Boltzmann流形。而目前的LLM是Boltzmann流形的特例。评论：18页主题：计算与语言(cs.CL)；统计力学(cond-mat.stat-mech)；高能物理-理论(hep-th)；微分几何(math.dg)MSC类：68T01 ACM类：I.2.7引用AS：arxiv：2407.21092cs.CL

[NLP-38] Accelerating Large Language Model Inference with Self-Supervised Early Exits
[NLP-38] 通过自我监督的早期退出加速大型语言模型推理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21082
作者: Florian Valade
关键词-EN: introducing early exits, paper presents, technique for accelerating, accelerating inference, introducing early
关键词-ZN: 引入早期退出，论文提出，加速技术，加速推理，引入早期
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Machine Learning (stat.ML)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This paper presents a novel technique for accelerating inference in large, pre-trained language models (LLMs) by introducing early exits during inference. The computational demands of these models, used across a wide range of applications, can be substantial. By capitalizing on the inherent variability in token complexity, our approach enables selective acceleration of the inference process. Specifically, we propose the integration of early exit ‘‘heads’’ atop existing transformer layers, which facilitate conditional terminations based on a confidence metric. These heads are trained in a self-supervised manner using the model’s own predictions as training data, thereby eliminating the need for additional annotated data. The confidence metric, established using a calibration set, ensures a desired level of accuracy while enabling early termination when confidence exceeds a predetermined threshold. Notably, our method preserves the original accuracy and reduces computational time on certain tasks, leveraging the existing knowledge of pre-trained LLMs without requiring extensive retraining. This lightweight, modular modification has the potential to greatly enhance the practical usability of LLMs, particularly in applications like real-time language processing in resource-constrained environments.
摘要：本文提出了一种在大型预训练语言模型(LLMS)中通过在推理过程中引入早期退出来加速推理的新技术。这些用于广泛应用的模型的计算需求可能是巨大的。通过利用令牌复杂性的内在可变性，我们的方法能够选择性地加速推理过程。具体地说，我们建议在现有的变压器层上集成早期退出“磁头”，这有助于基于置信度度量的条件终止。这些头部以自我监督的方式使用模型自己的预测作为训练数据进行训练，从而消除了对额外注释数据的需要。使用校准集建立的置信度度量确保了期望的准确度水平，同时允许在置信度超过预定阈值时提前终止。值得注意的是，我们的方法保持了原始的准确性，并减少了某些任务的计算时间，利用了预先训练的LLM的现有知识，而不需要大量的重新训练。这种轻量级的模块化修改有可能极大地增强LLMS的实际可用性，特别是在资源受限环境中的实时语言处理等应用中。

[NLP-39] Genetic Instruct: Scaling up Synthetic Generation of Coding Instructions for Large Language Models
[NLP-39] 遗传指令：扩大大型语言模型编码指令的合成生成

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21077
作者: Somshubra Majumdar,Vahid Noroozi,Sean Narenthiran,Aleksander Ficek,Jagadeesh Balam,Boris Ginsburg
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Large Language, Language Models, datasets poses challenges, creating these datasets
关键词-ZN: 大型语言模型、大型语言、语言模型、数据集在创建这些数据集时带来了挑战
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Neural and Evolutionary Computing (cs.NE)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) rely on instruction samples for alignment, but creating these datasets poses challenges, particularly in expert-dependent tasks like coding, which can be cost-prohibitive. One approach to mitigate these challenges is synthesizing data using another LLM. In this paper, we introduce a scalable method for generating synthetic instructions to enhance the code generation capability of LLMs. The proposed algorithm, Genetic-Instruct, mimics evolutionary processes, utilizing self-instruction to create numerous synthetic samples from a limited number of seeds. Genetic-Instruct is designed for efficient scaling of the generation process. Fine-tuning multiple coding LLMs with the synthetic samples demonstrates a significant improvement in their code generation accuracy compared to the baselines.
摘要：大型语言模型（LLM）依赖指令样本进行对齐，但创建这些数据集带来了挑战，特别是在编码等依赖专家的任务中，这可能成本过高。缓解这些挑战的一种方法是使用另一个LLM合成数据。本文中，我们介绍了一种可扩展的合成指令生成方法，以增强LLM的代码生成能力。提出的算法Genetic-Direct模仿进化过程，利用自我指令从有限数量的种子中创建大量合成样本。Genetic-Direct旨在有效扩展生成过程。与基线相比，使用合成样本微调多个编码LLM表明其代码生成准确性有显着提高。

[NLP-40] Apple Intelligence Foundation Language Models
[NLP-40] 苹果情报基金会语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21075
作者: Tom Gunter,Zirui Wang,Chong Wang,Ruoming Pang,Andy Narayanan,Aonan Zhang,Bowen Zhang,Chen Chen,Chung-Cheng Chiu,David Qiu,Deepak Gopinath,Dian Ang Yap,Dong Yin,Feng Nan,Floris Weers,Guoli Yin,Haoshuo Huang,Jianyu Wang,Jiarui Lu,John Peebles,Ke Ye,Mark Lee,Nan Du,Qibin Chen,Quentin Keunebroek,Sam Wiseman,Syd Evans,Tao Lei,Vivek Rathod,Xiang Kong,Xianzhi Du,Yanghao Li,Yongqiang Wang,Yuan Gao,Zaid Ahmed,Zhaoyang Xu,Zhiyun Lu,Al Rashid,Albin Madappally Jose,Alec Doane,Alfredo Bencomo,Allison Vanderby,Andrew Hansen,Ankur Jain,Anupama Mann Anupama,Areeba Kamal,Bugu Wu,Carolina Brum,Charlie Maalouf,Chinguun Erdenebileg,Chris Dulhanty,Dominik Moritz,Doug Kang,Eduardo Jimenez,Evan Ladd,Fangping Shi,Felix Bai,Frank Chu,Fred Hohman,Hadas Kotek,Hannah Gillis Coleman,Jane Li,Jeffrey Bigham,Jeffery Cao,Jeff Lai,Jessica Cheung,Jiulong Shan,Joe Zhou,John Li,Jun Qin,Karanjeet Singh,Karla Vega,Kelvin Zou,Laura Heckman,Lauren Gardiner,Margit Bowler,Maria Cordell,Meng Cao,Nicole Hay,Nilesh Shahdadpuri,Otto Godwin,Pranay Dighe,Pushyami Rachapudi,Ramsey Tantawi,Roman Frigg,Sam Davarnia,Sanskruti Shah,Saptarshi Guha,Sasha Sirovica,Shen Ma,Shuang Ma,Simon Wang,Sulgi Kim,Suma Jayaram,Vaishaal Shankar,Varsha Paidi,Vivek Kumar,Xin Wang,Xin Zheng,Walker Cheng
关键词-EN: Private Cloud Compute, Apple Intelligence features, power Apple Intelligence, Cloud Compute, present foundation language
关键词-ZN: 私有云计算、Apple Intelligence功能、强大Apple Intelligence、云计算、当前基础语言
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We present foundation language models developed to power Apple Intelligence features, including a ~3 billion parameter model designed to run efficiently on devices and a large server-based language model designed for Private Cloud Compute. These models are designed to perform a wide range of tasks efficiently, accurately, and responsibly. This report describes the model architecture, the data used to train the model, the training process, how the models are optimized for inference, and the evaluation results. We highlight our focus on Responsible AI and how the principles are applied throughout the model development.
摘要：我们介绍了为支持Apple Intelligence功能而开发的基础语言模型，包括旨在在设备上高效运行的约30亿个参数模型，以及专为私有云计算而设计的基于服务器的大型语言模型。这些模型旨在高效、准确和负责任地执行广泛的任务。该报告描述了模型架构、用于训练模型的数据、训练过程、如何优化模型以进行推理以及评估结果。我们强调了我们对负责任人工智能的关注以及如何在整个模型开发中应用这些原则。

[NLP-41] Enhancing Adversarial Text Attacks on BERT Models with Projected Gradient Descent
[NLP-41] 利用投影梯度下降增强BERT模型的对抗性文本攻击

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21073
作者: Hetvi Waghela,Jaydip Sen,Sneha Rakshit
关键词-EN: natural language processing, Projected Gradient Descent, deep learning models, learning models represent, integrating Projected Gradient
关键词-ZN: 自然语言处理、投影梯度下降、深度学习模型、学习模型表示、集成投影梯度
类目: Machine Learning (cs.LG); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Cryptography and Security (cs.CR)
备注: This paper is the pre-reviewed version of our paper that has been accepted for oral presentation and publication in the 4th IEEE ASIANCON. The conference will be organized in Pune, INDIA from August 23 to 25, 2024. The paper consists of 8 pages and it contains 10 tables. It is NOT the final camera-ready version that will be in IEEE Xplore

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Adversarial attacks against deep learning models represent a major threat to the security and reliability of natural language processing (NLP) systems. In this paper, we propose a modification to the BERT-Attack framework, integrating Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) to enhance its effectiveness and robustness. The original BERT-Attack, designed for generating adversarial examples against BERT-based models, suffers from limitations such as a fixed perturbation budget and a lack of consideration for semantic similarity. The proposed approach in this work, PGD-BERT-Attack, addresses these limitations by leveraging PGD to iteratively generate adversarial examples while ensuring both imperceptibility and semantic similarity to the original input. Extensive experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of PGD-BERT-Attack compared to the original BERT-Attack and other baseline methods. The results demonstrate that PGD-BERT-Attack achieves higher success rates in causing misclassification while maintaining low perceptual changes. Furthermore, PGD-BERT-Attack produces adversarial instances that exhibit greater semantic resemblance to the initial input, enhancing their applicability in real-world scenarios. Overall, the proposed modification offers a more effective and robust approach to adversarial attacks on BERT-based models, thus contributing to the advancement of defense against attacks on NLP systems.
摘要：针对深度学习模型的敌意攻击是对自然语言处理系统安全性和可靠性的主要威胁。在本文中，我们提出了一种改进的BERT攻击框架，结合投影梯度下降(PGD)来增强其有效性和稳健性。最初的BERT攻击旨在生成针对基于BERT的模型的敌意示例，但存在诸如固定的扰动预算和缺乏对语义相似性的考虑等限制。本文提出的方法PGD-BERT-Attack，通过利用PGD迭代生成对抗性实例，同时确保不可感知性和与原始输入的语义相似性，解决了这些限制。通过大量的实验，评估了PGD-BERT-攻击与原始BERT-攻击和其他基线方法的性能。实验结果表明，PGD-BERT-Attack在保持较低感知变化的情况下，具有较高的误分类成功率。此外，PGD-BERT-Attack生成的对抗性实例与初始输入具有更大的语义相似性，从而增强了它们在真实场景中的适用性。总体而言，拟议的修改提供了一种更有效和更健壮的方法来应对基于BERT的模型的对抗性攻击，从而有助于提高对NLP系统攻击的防御。

[NLP-42] Beyond Metrics: A Critical Analysis of the Variability in Large Language Model Evaluation Frameworks
[NLP-42] 超越预设：大型语言模型评估框架中变异性的批判性分析

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21072
作者: Marco AF Pimentel,Clément Christophe,Tathagata Raha,Prateek Munjal,Praveen K Kanithi,Shadab Khan
关键词-EN: continue to evolve, large language models, robust and standardized, standardized evaluation benchmarks, large language
关键词-ZN: 不断发展，大型语言模型，稳健且标准化，标准化的评估基准，大型语言
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 15 pages, 3 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:As large language models (LLMs) continue to evolve, the need for robust and standardized evaluation benchmarks becomes paramount. Evaluating the performance of these models is a complex challenge that requires careful consideration of various linguistic tasks, model architectures, and benchmarking methodologies. In recent years, various frameworks have emerged as noteworthy contributions to the field, offering comprehensive evaluation tests and benchmarks for assessing the capabilities of LLMs across diverse domains. This paper provides an exploration and critical analysis of some of these evaluation methodologies, shedding light on their strengths, limitations, and impact on advancing the state-of-the-art in natural language processing.
摘要：随着大型语言模型（LLM）的不断发展，对稳健和标准化的评估基准的需求变得至关重要。评估这些模型的性能是一项复杂的挑战，需要仔细考虑各种语言任务、模型架构和基准方法。近年来，各种框架对该领域做出了值得关注的贡献，提供了全面的评估测试和基准，用于评估不同领域的LLM的能力。本文对其中一些评估方法进行了探索和批判性分析，揭示了它们的优点、局限性以及对推进自然语言处理最新水平的影响。

[NLP-43] Occams Razor and Bender and Kollers Octopus ACL2024
[NLP-43] 奥卡姆·剃刀、本德和科勒斯章鱼

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21070
作者: Michael Guerzhoy
关键词-EN: Koller prominent ACL, discussion surrounding Bender, Climbing toward NLU, Bender and Koller, prominent ACL
关键词-ZN: Koller突出的ACL，围绕Bender的讨论，攀登NLU，Bender和Koller，突出的ACL
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: In ACL 2024 Workshop on Teaching NLP (TeachNLP 2024)

点击查看摘要

Abstract:We discuss the teaching of the discussion surrounding Bender and Koller’s prominent ACL 2020 paper, “Climbing toward NLU: on meaning form, and understanding in the age of data” \citebender2020climbing. We present what we understand to be the main contentions of the paper, and then recommend that the students engage with the natural counter-arguments to the claims in the paper. We attach teaching materials that we use to facilitate teaching this topic to undergraduate students.
摘要：我们讨论围绕Bender和Koller在ACL 2020年发表的著名论文“攀登NLU：关于意义形式和数据时代的理解”的讨论的教学。我们提出我们所理解的论文的主要论点，然后建议学生对论文中的主张进行自然的反驳。我们附上了我们用来促进向本科生教授该主题的教学材料。

[NLP-44] Exploring Genre and Success Classification through Song Lyrics using DistilBERT: A Fun NLP Venture
[NLP-44] 使用DistilBERT通过歌词探索流派和成功分类：一个有趣的NLP冒险

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21068
作者: Servando Pizarro Martinez,Moritz Zimmermann,Miguel Serkan Offermann,Florian Reither
关键词-EN: natural language processing, view-based success prediction, genre classification, release year prediction, approximate release year
关键词-ZN: 自然语言处理、基于视图的成功预测、类型分类、发布年预测、大致发布年
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This paper presents a natural language processing (NLP) approach to the problem of thoroughly comprehending song lyrics, with particular attention on genre classification, view-based success prediction, and approximate release year. Our tests provide promising results with 65% accuracy in genre classification and 79% accuracy in success prediction, leveraging a DistilBERT model for genre classification and BERT embeddings for release year prediction. Support Vector Machines outperformed other models in predicting the release year, achieving the lowest root mean squared error (RMSE) of 14.18. Our study offers insights that have the potential to revolutionize our relationship with music by addressing the shortcomings of current approaches in properly understanding the emotional intricacies of song lyrics.
摘要：本文提出了一种自然语言处理（NLP）方法来解决彻底理解歌曲歌词的问题，特别关注流派分类、基于视图的成功预测和大致发行年份。我们的测试提供了令人鼓舞的结果，流派分类的准确率为65%，成功预测的准确率为79%，利用DistilBERT模型进行流派分类，利用BERT嵌入进行发行年预测。支持载体机在预测发布年份方面优于其他模型，实现了14.18的最低均方误差（RSSE）。我们的研究提供了见解，通过解决当前正确理解歌词情感复杂性的方法的缺陷，有可能彻底改变我们与音乐的关系。

[NLP-45] ELP-Adapters: Parameter Efficient Adapter Tuning for Various Speech Processing Tasks
[NLP-45] ELP适配器：针对各种语音处理任务的参数高效适配器调整

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21066
作者: Nakamasa Inoue,Shinta Otake,Takumi Hirose,Masanari Ohi,Rei Kawakami
关键词-EN: Self-supervised learning, learning generic representations, learning generic, key approach, Self-supervised
关键词-ZN: 自我监督学习，学习类属表示，学习类属，关键方法，自我监督
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Self-supervised learning has emerged as a key approach for learning generic representations from speech data. Despite promising results in downstream tasks such as speech recognition, speaker verification, and emotion recognition, a significant number of parameters is required, which makes fine-tuning for each task memory-inefficient. To address this limitation, we introduce ELP-adapter tuning, a novel method for parameter-efficient fine-tuning using three types of adapter, namely encoder adapters (E-adapters), layer adapters (L-adapters), and a prompt adapter (P-adapter). The E-adapters are integrated into transformer-based encoder layers and help to learn fine-grained speech representations that are effective for speech recognition. The L-adapters create paths from each encoder layer to the downstream head and help to extract non-linguistic features from lower encoder layers that are effective for speaker verification and emotion recognition. The P-adapter appends pseudo features to CNN features to further improve effectiveness and efficiency. With these adapters, models can be quickly adapted to various speech processing tasks. Our evaluation across four downstream tasks using five backbone models demonstrated the effectiveness of the proposed method. With the WavLM backbone, its performance was comparable to or better than that of full fine-tuning on all tasks while requiring 90% fewer learnable parameters.
摘要：自监督学习已经成为从语音数据中学习泛型表示的一种重要方法。尽管在语音识别、说话人验证和情感识别等下游任务中取得了令人振奋的结果，但需要大量的参数，这使得对每个任务的微调都是低效的。针对这一局限性，我们引入了ELP适配器调整方法，该方法使用三种类型的适配器，即编码器适配器(E-适配器)、层适配器(L-适配器)和提示适配器(P-适配器)来进行参数高效微调。E适配器集成到基于变压器的编码层中，有助于学习对语音识别有效的细粒度语音表示。L适配器创建从每个编码层到下游头部的路径，并帮助从较低的编码层提取非语言特征，这些特征对于说话人验证和情感识别是有效的。P-Adapter将伪特征添加到CNN特征中，以进一步提高有效性和效率。有了这些适配器，模型可以快速适应各种语音处理任务。我们使用五个主干模型对四个下游任务进行了评估，证明了所提出的方法的有效性。使用WavLM主干，其性能与在所有任务上完全微调的性能相当或更好，而需要的可学习参数减少90%。

[NLP-46] LawLLM: Law Large Language Model for the US Legal System CIKM2024
[NLP-46] LawLLM：美国法律体系的法律大语言模型

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21065
作者: Dong Shu,Haoran Zhao,Xukun Liu,David Demeter,Mengnan Du,Yongfeng Zhang
关键词-EN: rapidly evolving field, accurately predicting judicial, predicting judicial outcomes, finding relevant cases, includes specialized terminology
关键词-ZN: 快速发展的领域，准确预测司法，预测司法结果，查找相关案件，包括专业术语
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 21 pages, 2 figures, accepted at the 33rd ACM International Conference on Information and Knowledge Management (CIKM 2024) for the Applied Research Paper track

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In the rapidly evolving field of legal analytics, finding relevant cases and accurately predicting judicial outcomes are challenging because of the complexity of legal language, which often includes specialized terminology, complex syntax, and historical context. Moreover, the subtle distinctions between similar and precedent cases require a deep understanding of legal knowledge. Researchers often conflate these concepts, making it difficult to develop specialized techniques to effectively address these nuanced tasks. In this paper, we introduce the Law Large Language Model (LawLLM), a multi-task model specifically designed for the US legal domain to address these challenges. LawLLM excels at Similar Case Retrieval (SCR), Precedent Case Recommendation (PCR), and Legal Judgment Prediction (LJP). By clearly distinguishing between precedent and similar cases, we provide essential clarity, guiding future research in developing specialized strategies for these tasks. We propose customized data preprocessing techniques for each task that transform raw legal data into a trainable format. Furthermore, we also use techniques such as in-context learning (ICL) and advanced information retrieval methods in LawLLM. The evaluation results demonstrate that LawLLM consistently outperforms existing baselines in both zero-shot and few-shot scenarios, offering unparalleled multi-task capabilities and filling critical gaps in the legal domain.
摘要：在快速发展的法律分析领域，由于法律语言的复杂性，查找相关案例和准确预测司法结果是具有挑战性的，因为法律语言往往包括专业术语、复杂的句法和历史背景。此外，类似案件和先例之间的细微区别需要对法律知识有深刻的理解。研究人员经常将这些概念混为一谈，这使得开发专门的技术来有效地处理这些细微差别的任务变得困难。在本文中，我们介绍了法律大型语言模型(LawLLM)，这是一个专门为美国法律领域设计的多任务模型，以应对这些挑战。LawLLM擅长于相似案例检索(SCR)、判例推荐(PCR)和法律判决预测(LJP)。通过明确区分先例和类似案例，我们提供了必要的明确性，指导未来的研究为这些任务制定专门的战略。我们为每个任务提出了定制的数据预处理技术，将原始法律数据转换为可训练的格式。此外，我们还在LawLLM中使用了上下文中学习(ICL)和高级信息检索方法等技术。评估结果表明，LawLLM在零射击和少射击场景中的表现始终优于现有的基线，提供无与伦比的多任务能力，并填补了法律领域的关键空白。

[NLP-47] Improving noisy student training for low-resource languages in End-to-End ASR using CycleGAN and inter-domain losses LREC COLING2024
[NLP-47] 使用CycleGAN和域间损失改善端到端ASB中低资源语言的嘈杂学生培训

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21061
作者: Chia-Yu Li,Ngoc Thang Vu
关键词-EN: speech recognition system, significantly improved performance, inter-domain losses, speech recognition, speech
关键词-ZN: 语音识别系统，性能显着提高，域间损失，语音识别，语音
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: 10 pages (2 for references), 4 figures, published in SIGUL2024@LREC-COLING 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Training a semi-supervised end-to-end speech recognition system using noisy student training has significantly improved performance. However, this approach requires a substantial amount of paired speech-text and unlabeled speech, which is costly for low-resource languages. Therefore, this paper considers a more extreme case of semi-supervised end-to-end automatic speech recognition where there are limited paired speech-text, unlabeled speech (less than five hours), and abundant external text. Firstly, we observe improved performance by training the model using our previous work on semi-supervised learning “CycleGAN and inter-domain losses” solely with external text. Secondly, we enhance “CycleGAN and inter-domain losses” by incorporating automatic hyperparameter tuning, calling it “enhanced CycleGAN inter-domain losses.” Thirdly, we integrate it into the noisy student training approach pipeline for low-resource scenarios. Our experimental results, conducted on six non-English languages from Voxforge and Common Voice, show a 20% word error rate reduction compared to the baseline teacher model and a 10% word error rate reduction compared to the baseline best student model, highlighting the significant improvements achieved through our proposed method.
摘要：利用有噪声的学生训练半监督端到端语音识别系统，显著提高了系统的性能。然而，这种方法需要大量成对的语音-文本和未标记的语音，这对于低资源语言来说是昂贵的。因此，本文考虑了一种更极端的半监督端到端自动语音识别的情况，其中存在有限的语音-文本配对、未标记的语音(不到5个小时)和大量的外部文本。首先，我们观察到，通过使用我们之前在半监督学习“CycleGAN和域间损失”方面的工作，仅使用外部文本训练模型，性能有所改善。其次，我们通过加入自动超参数调整来增强“CycleGAN和域间损失”，称之为“增强的CycleGAN域间损失”。第三，我们将其集成到针对低资源场景的嘈杂的学生培训方法管道中。我们在Voxforge和Common Voice的六种非英语语言上进行的实验结果显示，与基准教师模型相比，单词错误率降低了20%，与基准最佳学生模型相比，单词错误率降低了10%，突出了我们所提出的方法所取得的显著改进。

[NLP-48] Using Large Language Models for the Interpretation of Building Regulations
[NLP-48] 使用大型语言模型解释建筑法规

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21060
作者: Stefan Fuchs,Michael Witbrock,Johannes Dimyadi,Robert Amor
关键词-EN: Compliance checking, automated compliance checking, essential part, construction project, Compliance
关键词-ZN: 合规性检查，自动合规性检查，重要部件，建设项目，合规性
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: Presented at the 13th Conference on Engineering, Project and Production Management

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Compliance checking is an essential part of a construction project. The recent rapid uptake of building information models (BIM) in the construction industry has created more opportunities for automated compliance checking (ACC). BIM enables sharing of digital building design data that can be used for compliance checking with legal requirements, which are conventionally conveyed in natural language and not intended for machine processing. Creating a computable representation of legal requirements suitable for ACC is complex, costly, and time-consuming. Large language models (LLMs) such as the generative pre-trained transformers (GPT), GPT-3.5 and GPT-4, powering OpenAI’s ChatGPT, can generate logically coherent text and source code responding to user prompts. This capability could be used to automate the conversion of building regulations into a semantic and computable representation. This paper evaluates the performance of LLMs in translating building regulations into LegalRuleML in a few-shot learning setup. By providing GPT-3.5 with only a few example translations, it can learn the basic structure of the format. Using a system prompt, we further specify the LegalRuleML representation and explore the existence of expert domain knowledge in the model. Such domain knowledge might be ingrained in GPT-3.5 through the broad pre-training but needs to be brought forth by careful contextualisation. Finally, we investigate whether strategies such as chain-of-thought reasoning and self-consistency could apply to this use case. As LLMs become more sophisticated, the increased common sense, logical coherence, and means to domain adaptation can significantly support ACC, leading to more efficient and effective checking processes.
摘要：合规性检查是建设项目的重要组成部分。最近建筑信息模型(BIM)在建筑业的迅速普及为自动化合规性检查(ACC)创造了更多的机会。BIM实现了数字建筑设计数据的共享，这些数据可用于符合法律要求的合规性检查，这些要求通常以自然语言传达，不打算用于机器处理。创建适用于ACC的法律要求的可计算表示法是复杂、昂贵和耗时的。大型语言模型(LLM)，如生成性预训练转换器(GPT)、GPT-3.5和GPT-4，支持OpenAI的ChatGPT，可以生成逻辑连贯的文本和源代码，以响应用户提示。这一功能可用于自动将建筑规则转换为语义和可计算的表示形式。本文评估了LLMS在将建筑法规转换为LegalRuleML方面的性能，在几次学习设置中。通过向GPT-3.5提供几个示例翻译，它可以了解该格式的基本结构。使用系统提示符，我们进一步指定了LegalRuleML表示，并探索了模型中专家领域知识的存在。这样的领域知识可能通过广泛的预培训在GPT-3.5中根深蒂固，但需要通过仔细的语境来带来。最后，我们调查了思想链推理和自我一致性等策略是否适用于这个用例。随着LLMS变得更加复杂，增加的常识、逻辑一致性和领域适应方法可以显著支持ACC，从而导致更高效和有效的检查过程。

[NLP-49] Modular RAG: Transforming RAG Systems into LEGO-like Reconfigurable Frameworks
[NLP-49] 模块化RAG：将RAG系统转变为类似乐高的可重新配置框架

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21059
作者: Yunfan Gao,Yun Xiong,Meng Wang,Haofen Wang
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Retrieval-augmented Generation, Language Models, Large Language, tackling knowledge-intensive tasks
关键词-ZN: 大型语言模型、检索增强生成、语言模型、大型语言、处理知识密集型任务
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Information Retrieval (cs.IR)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Retrieval-augmented Generation (RAG) has markedly enhanced the capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) in tackling knowledge-intensive tasks. The increasing demands of application scenarios have driven the evolution of RAG, leading to the integration of advanced retrievers, LLMs and other complementary technologies, which in turn has amplified the intricacy of RAG systems. However, the rapid advancements are outpacing the foundational RAG paradigm, with many methods struggling to be unified under the process of “retrieve-then-generate”. In this context, this paper examines the limitations of the existing RAG paradigm and introduces the modular RAG framework. By decomposing complex RAG systems into independent modules and specialized operators, it facilitates a highly reconfigurable framework. Modular RAG transcends the traditional linear architecture, embracing a more advanced design that integrates routing, scheduling, and fusion mechanisms. Drawing on extensive research, this paper further identifies prevalent RAG patterns-linear, conditional, branching, and looping-and offers a comprehensive analysis of their respective implementation nuances. Modular RAG presents innovative opportunities for the conceptualization and deployment of RAG systems. Finally, the paper explores the potential emergence of new operators and paradigms, establishing a solid theoretical foundation and a practical roadmap for the continued evolution and practical deployment of RAG technologies.
摘要：检索增强生成(RAG)显著提高了大型语言模型(LLM)处理知识密集型任务的能力。不断增长的应用场景需求推动了RAG的发展，导致了高级检索器、LLMS和其他补充技术的集成，这反过来又放大了RAG系统的复杂性。然而，快速发展的速度正在超过基本的RAG范式，许多方法在“检索-然后-生成”的过程中难以统一。在此背景下，本文研究了现有RAG范式的局限性，并介绍了模块化RAG框架。通过将复杂的RAG系统分解成独立的模块和专门的操作符，它促进了高度可重新配置的框架。模块化RAG超越了传统的线性架构，采用了集成了路由、调度和融合机制的更高级设计。在广泛研究的基础上，本文进一步确定了流行的RAG模式-线性、条件、分支和循环-并对它们各自的实现细微差别进行了全面的分析。模块化RAG为RAG系统的概念化和部署提供了创新的机会。最后，本文探讨了新的运算符和范式的潜在出现，为RAG技术的继续演进和实际部署奠定了坚实的理论基础和实践路线图。

[NLP-50] Understanding the Interplay of Scale Data and Bias in Language Models: A Case Study with BERT
[NLP-50] 了解语言模型中规模数据和偏差的相互作用：BERT的案例研究

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21058
作者: Muhammad Ali,Swetasudha Panda,Qinlan Shen,Michael Wick,Ari Kobren
关键词-EN: larger datasets, advance towards intelligence, language model research, current landscape, model
关键词-ZN: 更大的数据集、智能进步、语言模型研究、当前格局、模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In the current landscape of language model research, larger models, larger datasets and more compute seems to be the only way to advance towards intelligence. While there have been extensive studies of scaling laws and models’ scaling behaviors, the effect of scale on a model’s social biases and stereotyping tendencies has received less attention. In this study, we explore the influence of model scale and pre-training data on its learnt social biases. We focus on BERT – an extremely popular language model – and investigate biases as they show up during language modeling (upstream), as well as during classification applications after fine-tuning (downstream). Our experiments on four architecture sizes of BERT demonstrate that pre-training data substantially influences how upstream biases evolve with model scale. With increasing scale, models pre-trained on large internet scrapes like Common Crawl exhibit higher toxicity, whereas models pre-trained on moderated data sources like Wikipedia show greater gender stereotypes. However, downstream biases generally decrease with increasing model scale, irrespective of the pre-training data. Our results highlight the qualitative role of pre-training data in the biased behavior of language models, an often overlooked aspect in the study of scale. Through a detailed case study of BERT, we shed light on the complex interplay of data and model scale, and investigate how it translates to concrete biases.
摘要：在当前语言模型研究的格局中，更大的模型、更大的数据集和更多的计算似乎是迈向智能的唯一途径。虽然已有大量关于尺度规律和模型尺度行为的研究，但尺度对模型的社会偏见和刻板印象倾向的影响却较少受到关注。在本研究中，我们探讨了模型规模和训练前数据对其习得的社会偏见的影响。我们将重点放在ERT–一个非常流行的语言模型上–并调查在语言建模期间(上游)以及在微调后的分类应用程序中(下游)出现的偏差。我们在四种架构规模的BERT上的实验表明，预训练数据显著影响上游偏差随模型规模的演变。随着规模的扩大，针对Common Crawl等大型互联网爬虫进行预培训的模型显示出更高的毒性，而针对维基百科等温和数据源进行预培训的模型显示出更大的性别刻板印象。然而，无论训练前的数据如何，下游偏差通常随着模型规模的增加而减小。我们的结果突出了训练前数据在语言模型偏向行为中的定性作用，这是量表研究中经常被忽视的一个方面。通过对BERT的详细案例研究，我们阐明了数据和模型规模之间的复杂相互作用，并研究了它如何转化为具体的偏差。

[NLP-51] Multi-group Uncertainty Quantification for Long-form Text Generation
[NLP-51] 长格式文本生成的多组不确定性量化

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21057
作者: Terrance Liu,Zhiwei Steven Wu
关键词-EN: consumer-facing applications, rapidly moving, moving towards consumer-facing, large language models, large language
关键词-ZN: 面向消费者的应用程序，快速发展，转向面向消费者，大型语言模型，大型语言
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:While large language models are rapidly moving towards consumer-facing applications, they are often still prone to factual errors and hallucinations. In order to reduce the potential harms that may come from these errors, it is important for users to know to what extent they can trust an LLM when it makes a factual claim. To this end, we study the problem of uncertainty quantification of factual correctness in long-form natural language generation. Given some output from a large language model, we study both uncertainty at the level of individual claims contained within the output (via calibration) and uncertainty across the entire output itself (via conformal prediction). Moreover, we invoke multicalibration and multivalid conformal prediction to ensure that such uncertainty guarantees are valid both marginally and across distinct groups of prompts. Using the task of biography generation, we demonstrate empirically that having access to and making use of additional group attributes for each prompt improves both overall and group-wise performance. As the problems of calibration, conformal prediction, and their multi-group counterparts have not been extensively explored previously in the context of long-form text generation, we consider these empirical results to form a benchmark for this setting.
摘要：虽然大型语言模型正在迅速转向面向消费者的应用程序，但它们仍然经常容易出现事实错误和幻觉。为了减少这些错误可能带来的潜在危害，重要的是让用户知道当LLM做出事实声明时，他们可以在多大程度上信任它。为此，我们研究了长形自然语言生成中事实正确性的不确定性量化问题。给出一个大型语言模型的一些输出，我们既研究输出中包含的个别声明水平的不确定性(通过校准)，也研究整个输出本身的不确定性(通过保角预测)。此外，我们使用多校准和多有效共形预测来确保这样的不确定性保证在边缘和跨不同的提示组有效。使用传记生成任务，我们实证地证明，访问和利用每个提示的附加组属性可以提高总体和组方面的绩效。由于校准、共形预测及其多组对应的问题以前没有在长格式文本生成的背景下被广泛探索，我们认为这些经验结果构成了这种设置的基准。

[NLP-52] What Matters in Explanations: Towards Explainable Fake Review Detection Focusing on Transformers
[NLP-52] 解释中重要的是：迈向可解释的虚假评论检测，聚焦于变形金刚

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21056
作者: Md Shajalal,Md Atabuzzaman,Alexander Boden,Gunnar Stevens,Delong Du
关键词-EN: feedback play crucial, play crucial role, customers’ purchasing decisions, platforms like Amazon, customers’ purchasing
关键词-ZN: 反馈发挥着至关重要的作用，客户的购买决策，亚马逊等平台，客户的购买
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Social and Information Networks (cs.SI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Customers’ reviews and feedback play crucial role on electronic commerce~(E-commerce) platforms like Amazon, Zalando, and eBay in influencing other customers’ purchasing decisions. However, there is a prevailing concern that sellers often post fake or spam reviews to deceive potential customers and manipulate their opinions about a product. Over the past decade, there has been considerable interest in using machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models to identify such fraudulent reviews. Unfortunately, the decisions made by complex ML and DL models - which often function as \emphblack-boxes - can be surprising and difficult for general users to comprehend. In this paper, we propose an explainable framework for detecting fake reviews with high precision in identifying fraudulent content with explanations and investigate what information matters most for explaining particular decisions by conducting empirical user evaluation. Initially, we develop fake review detection models using DL and transformer models including XLNet and DistilBERT. We then introduce layer-wise relevance propagation (LRP) technique for generating explanations that can map the contributions of words toward the predicted class. The experimental results on two benchmark fake review detection datasets demonstrate that our predictive models achieve state-of-the-art performance and outperform several existing methods. Furthermore, the empirical user evaluation of the generated explanations concludes which important information needs to be considered in generating explanations in the context of fake review identification.
摘要：在亚马逊、Zalando和eBay等电子商务平台上，客户的评论和反馈在影响其他客户的购买决策方面发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，人们普遍担心，卖家经常发布虚假或垃圾评论，以欺骗潜在客户，并操纵他们对产品的看法。在过去的十年中，人们对使用机器学习(ML)和深度学习(DL)模型来识别此类欺诈性评论产生了相当大的兴趣。不幸的是，由复杂的ML和DL模型做出的决策–它们通常起着黑匣子的作用–可能会让普通用户感到惊讶和难以理解。在本文中，我们提出了一个可解释的框架，用于在识别带有解释的欺诈性内容时高精度地检测虚假评论，并通过进行经验性的用户评估来研究哪些信息对解释特定决策最重要。首先，我们使用DL和包括XLNet和DistilBERT在内的转换器模型来开发虚假评论检测模型。然后，我们引入了分层关联传播(LRP)技术来生成解释，该解释可以将单词的贡献映射到预测的类。在两个基准虚假评论检测数据集上的实验结果表明，我们的预测模型达到了最先进的性能，并优于现有的几种方法。此外，对生成的解释的经验性用户评估得出在虚假评论识别的背景下生成解释时需要考虑哪些重要信息。

[NLP-53] Bailicai: A Domain-Optimized Retrieval-Augmented Generation Framework for Medical Applications
[NLP-53] 百利财：一个面向医疗应用的领域优化检索增强生成框架

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21055
作者: Cui Long,Yongbin Liu,Chunping Ouyang,Ying Yu
关键词-EN: prompting extensive exploration, exhibited remarkable proficiency, natural language understanding, Large Language Models, prompting extensive
关键词-ZN: 促进了广泛的探索，表现出非凡的熟练程度、自然语言理解、大型语言模型，促进了广泛的探索
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Large Language Models (LLMs) have exhibited remarkable proficiency in natural language understanding, prompting extensive exploration of their potential applications across diverse domains. In the medical domain, open-source LLMs have demonstrated moderate efficacy following domain-specific fine-tuning; however, they remain substantially inferior to proprietary models such as GPT-4 and GPT-3.5. These open-source models encounter limitations in the comprehensiveness of domain-specific knowledge and exhibit a propensity for ‘hallucinations’ during text generation. To mitigate these issues, researchers have implemented the Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach, which augments LLMs with background information from external knowledge bases while preserving the model’s internal parameters. However, document noise can adversely affect performance, and the application of RAG in the medical field remains in its nascent stages. This study presents the Bailicai framework: a novel integration of retrieval-augmented generation with large language models optimized for the medical domain. The Bailicai framework augments the performance of LLMs in medicine through the implementation of four sub-modules. Experimental results demonstrate that the Bailicai approach surpasses existing medical domain LLMs across multiple medical benchmarks and exceeds the performance of GPT-3.5. Furthermore, the Bailicai method effectively attenuates the prevalent issue of hallucinations in medical applications of LLMs and ameliorates the noise-related challenges associated with traditional RAG techniques when processing irrelevant or pseudo-relevant documents.
摘要：大型语言模型在自然语言理解方面表现出了卓越的能力，促使人们广泛探索它们在不同领域的潜在应用。在医疗领域，经过特定领域的微调后，开源LLM已显示出中等的疗效；然而，它们仍然显著低于专有模型，如GPT-4和GPT-3.5。这些开源模型在领域特定知识的全面性方面遇到了限制，并在文本生成过程中表现出“幻觉”的倾向。为了缓解这些问题，研究人员实现了检索-增强生成(RAG)方法，该方法在保留模型内部参数的同时，使用来自外部知识库的背景信息来增强LLM。然而，文档噪声可能会对性能产生不利影响，RAG在医疗领域的应用仍处于初级阶段。这项研究提出了百利才框架：一种新的整合了检索-增强生成和针对医学领域优化的大型语言模型的框架。百里彩框架通过实现四个子模块来增强医学领域的LLMS的性能。实验结果表明，该方法在多个医学基准上都超过了现有的医学领域LLMS，并超过了GPT-3.5的性能。此外，Bailicai方法有效地缓解了LLMS医疗应用中普遍存在的幻觉问题，并改善了传统RAG技术在处理无关或伪相关文档时与噪声相关的挑战。

[NLP-54] Sentiment Reasoning for Healthcare
[NLP-54] 医疗保健的情感推理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21054
作者: Khai Le-Duc,Khai-Nguyen Nguyen,Bach Phan Tat,Duy Le,Jerry Ngo,Long Vo-Dang,Anh Totti Nguyen,Truong-Son Hy
关键词-EN: sentiment analysis task, Large Language Models, consequences of errors, decision-making is crucial, crucial in healthcare
关键词-ZN: 情感分析任务、大型语言模型、错误的后果、决策至关重要，在医疗保健中至关重要
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG); Sound (cs.SD); Audio and Speech Processing (eess.AS)
备注: Preprint, 18 pages

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Transparency in AI decision-making is crucial in healthcare due to the severe consequences of errors, and this is important for building trust among AI and users in sentiment analysis task. Incorporating reasoning capabilities helps Large Language Models (LLMs) understand human emotions within broader contexts, handle nuanced and ambiguous language, and infer underlying sentiments that may not be explicitly stated. In this work, we introduce a new task - Sentiment Reasoning - for both speech and text modalities, along with our proposed multimodal multitask framework and dataset. Our study showed that rationale-augmented training enhances model performance in sentiment classification across both human transcript and ASR settings. Also, we found that the generated rationales typically exhibit different vocabularies compared to human-generated rationales, but maintain similar semantics. All code, data (English-translated and Vietnamese) and models are published online: this https URL
摘要：由于错误的严重后果，人工智能决策的透明度在医疗保健中至关重要，这对于在情感分析任务中建立人工智能和用户之间的信任非常重要。结合推理能力有助于大型语言模型(LLM)在更广泛的上下文中理解人类情感，处理细微和模棱两可的语言，并推断可能没有明确表达的潜在情感。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种新的任务-情感推理-语音和文本通道，以及我们提出的多通道多任务框架和数据集。我们的研究表明，理性增强的训练提高了模型在人类转录和ASR环境下的情感分类性能。此外，我们发现，与人类生成的理由相比，生成的理由通常表现出不同的词汇，但保持了相似的语义。所有代码、数据(英语翻译和越南语)和模型均在线发布：此HTTPS URL

[NLP-55] Knowledge Models for Cancer Clinical Practice Guidelines : Construction Management and Usage in Question Answering
[NLP-55] 癌症临床实践指南的知识模型：问题解答中的构建管理和使用

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21053
作者: Pralaypati Ta,Bhumika Gupta,Arihant Jain,Sneha Sree C,Keerthi Ram,Mohanasankar Sivaprakasam
关键词-EN: Cancer Clinical Practice, Clinical Practice Guidelines, Clinical Practice, minimal human intervention, Cancer Clinical
关键词-ZN: 癌症临床实践，临床实践指南，临床实践，最低限度的人为干预，癌症临床
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:An automated knowledge modeling algorithm for Cancer Clinical Practice Guidelines (CPGs) extracts the knowledge contained in the CPG documents and transforms it into a programmatically interactable, easy-to-update structured model with minimal human intervention. The existing automated algorithms have minimal scope and cannot handle the varying complexity of the knowledge content in the CPGs for different cancer types. This work proposes an improved automated knowledge modeling algorithm to create knowledge models from the National Comprehensive Cancer Network (NCCN) CPGs in Oncology for different cancer types. The proposed algorithm has been evaluated with NCCN CPGs for four different cancer types. We also proposed an algorithm to compare the knowledge models for different versions of a guideline to discover the specific changes introduced in the treatment protocol of a new version. We created a question-answering (QA) framework with the guideline knowledge models as the augmented knowledge base to study our ability to query the knowledge models. We compiled a set of 32 question-answer pairs derived from two reliable data sources for the treatment of Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC) to evaluate the QA framework. The framework was evaluated against the question-answer pairs from one data source, and it can generate the answers with 54.5% accuracy from the treatment algorithm and 81.8% accuracy from the discussion part of the NCCN NSCLC guideline knowledge model.
摘要：癌症临床实践指南(CPGs)的自动知识建模算法提取CPG文档中包含的知识，并将其转换为可编程交互的、易于更新的结构化模型，只需最少的人工干预。现有的自动化算法范围很小，不能处理不同癌症类型的CPG中知识内容的不同复杂性。这项工作提出了一种改进的自动化知识建模算法，以创建来自国家癌症综合网络(NCCN)肿瘤学CPG的不同癌症类型的知识模型。对于四种不同的癌症类型，我们用NCCN的CPG对该算法进行了评估。我们还提出了一个算法来比较不同版本指南的知识模型，以发现新版本的治疗方案中引入的具体变化。我们创建了一个以指南知识模型为补充知识库的问答框架，以研究我们对知识模型的查询能力。我们汇编了一组32个问答对，来自两个可靠的数据来源，用于非小细胞肺癌(NSCLC)的治疗，以评估QA框架。该框架针对来自同一数据源的问答对进行了评估，处理算法生成的答案正确率为54.5%，NCCN NSCLC指南知识模型讨论部分的正确率为81.8%。

[NLP-56] able-Filling via Mean Teacher for Cross-domain Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction CIKM2024
[NLP-56] 跨域方面情感三重组提取的均值教师可填充

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21052
作者: Kun Peng,Lei Jiang,Qian Li,Haoran Li,Xiaoyan Yu,Li Sun,Shuo Sun,Yanxian Bi,Hao Peng
关键词-EN: Aspect Sentiment Triplet, Sentiment Triplet Extraction, Cross-domain Aspect Sentiment, extract fine-grained sentiment, fine-grained sentiment elements
关键词-ZN: 方面情感三重组，情感三重组提取，跨领域方面情感，提取细粒度情感，细粒度情感元素
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: Accepted by CIKM2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Cross-domain Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction (ASTE) aims to extract fine-grained sentiment elements from target domain sentences by leveraging the knowledge acquired from the source domain. Due to the absence of labeled data in the target domain, recent studies tend to rely on pre-trained language models to generate large amounts of synthetic data for training purposes. However, these approaches entail additional computational costs associated with the generation process. Different from them, we discover a striking resemblance between table-filling methods in ASTE and two-stage Object Detection (OD) in computer vision, which inspires us to revisit the cross-domain ASTE task and approach it from an OD standpoint. This allows the model to benefit from the OD extraction paradigm and region-level alignment. Building upon this premise, we propose a novel method named \textbfTable-\textbfFilling via \textbfMean \textbfTeacher (TFMT). Specifically, the table-filling methods encode the sentence into a 2D table to detect word relations, while TFMT treats the table as a feature map and utilizes a region consistency to enhance the quality of those generated pseudo labels. Additionally, considering the existence of the domain gap, a cross-domain consistency based on Maximum Mean Discrepancy is designed to alleviate domain shift problems. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance with minimal parameters and computational costs, making it a strong baseline for cross-domain ASTE.
摘要：跨域方面情感三元组提取(ASE)旨在利用从源域获取的知识，从目标领域句子中提取细粒度的情感元素。由于目标领域缺乏标记数据，最近的研究往往依赖于预先训练的语言模型来生成大量用于训练的合成数据。然而，这些方法需要与生成过程相关的额外计算成本。与它们不同的是，我们发现ASTE中的填表方法与计算机视觉中的两阶段目标检测(OD)有着惊人的相似之处，这促使我们重新审视跨域ASTE任务，并从OD的角度来处理它。这使得模型能够受益于OD提取范例和区域级别的对齐。在此前提下，我们提出了一种新的方法TFMT(TFMT)，称为TFMT。具体地，填表方法将句子编码成二维表以检测词关系，而TFMT将该表视为特征映射，并利用区域一致性来提高生成的伪标签的质量。此外，考虑到区域间隙的存在，设计了一种基于最大均值差异的跨域一致性来缓解区域移位问题。我们的方法以最小的参数和计算成本实现了最先进的性能，使其成为跨域ASE的强大基线。

[NLP-57] An Active Inference Strategy for Prompting Reliable Responses from Large Language Models in Medical Practice
[NLP-57] 在医疗实践中从大型语言模型中提取可靠响应的主动推理策略

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21051
作者: Roma Shusterman,Allison C. Waters,Shannon O`Neill,Phan Luu,Don M. Tucker
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Language Models, Large Language, artificial intelligence offer, intelligence offer important
关键词-ZN: 大型语言模型，语言模型，大型语言，人工智能提供，智能提供重要
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 25 pages, 4 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Continuing advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) in artificial intelligence offer important capacities in intuitively accessing and using medical knowledge in many contexts, including education and training as well as assessment and treatment. Most of the initial literature on LLMs in medicine has emphasized that LLMs are unsuitable for medical use because they are non-deterministic, may provide incorrect or harmful responses, and cannot be regulated to assure quality control. If these issues could be corrected, optimizing LLM technology could benefit patients and physicians by providing affordable, point-of-care medical knowledge. Our proposed framework refines LLM responses by restricting their primary knowledge base to domain-specific datasets containing validated medical information. Additionally, we introduce an actor-critic LLM prompting protocol based on active inference principles of human cognition, where a Therapist agent initially responds to patient queries, and a Supervisor agent evaluates and adjusts responses to ensure accuracy and reliability. We conducted a validation study where expert cognitive behaviour therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) therapists evaluated responses from the LLM in a blind format. Experienced human CBT-I therapists assessed responses to 100 patient queries, comparing LLM-generated responses with appropriate and inappropriate responses crafted by experienced CBT-I therapists. Results showed that LLM responses received high ratings from the CBT-I therapists, often exceeding those of therapist-generated appropriate responses. This structured approach aims to integrate advanced LLM technology into medical applications, meeting regulatory requirements for establishing the safe and effective use of special purpose validated LLMs in medicine.
摘要：人工智能领域中大型语言模型(LLM)的不断发展为在教育和培训以及评估和治疗等许多环境中直观地获取和使用医学知识提供了重要的能力。大多数医学上关于LLM的最初文献都强调LLM不适合医疗用途，因为它们是非确定性的，可能提供不正确或有害的反应，并且不能被监管以确保质量控制。如果这些问题能够得到纠正，优化LLM技术可以通过提供负担得起的临床点医学知识来使患者和医生受益。我们提出的框架通过将LLM响应的主要知识库限制在包含有效医疗信息的特定领域数据集来优化LLM响应。此外，我们引入了一种基于人类认知的主动推理原理的行动者-批评者LLM提示协议，其中治疗师代理最初响应患者的询问，而主管代理评估和调整响应以确保准确性和可靠性。我们进行了一项验证性研究，其中专家认知行为治疗失眠(CBT-I)治疗师以盲法评估LLM的反应。经验丰富的人类CBT-I治疗师评估了对100名患者询问的反应，将LLM生成的反应与经验丰富的CBT-I治疗师制定的适当和不适当的反应进行了比较。结果表明，CBT-I治疗师对LLM反应的评分很高，经常超过治疗师产生的适当反应。这种结构化的方法旨在将先进的LLM技术集成到医疗应用中，满足在医学中安全有效地使用特殊目的验证的LLM的法规要求。

[NLP-58] Artificial Intelligence in Extracting Diagnostic Data from Dental Records
[NLP-58] 人工智能从牙科记录中提取诊断数据

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21050
作者: Yao-Shun Chuang,Chun-Teh Lee,Oluwabunmi Tokede,Guo-Hao Lin,Ryan Brandon,Trung Duong Tran,Xiaoqian Jiang,Muhammad F. Walji
关键词-EN: unstructured text, missing structured, missing structured data, missing structured diagnoses, Site
关键词-ZN: 非结构化文本、缺失结构化、缺失结构化数据、缺失结构化诊断、网站
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL)
备注: 11 pages, 2 tables, 3 figures, under review

点击查看摘要

Abstract:This research addresses the issue of missing structured data in dental records by extracting diagnostic information from unstructured text. The updated periodontology classification system’s complexity has increased incomplete or missing structured diagnoses. To tackle this, we use advanced AI and NLP methods, leveraging GPT-4 to generate synthetic notes for fine-tuning a RoBERTa model. This significantly enhances the model’s ability to understand medical and dental language. We evaluated the model using 120 randomly selected clinical notes from two datasets, demonstrating its improved diagnostic extraction accuracy. The results showed high accuracy in diagnosing periodontal status, stage, and grade, with Site 1 scoring 0.99 and Site 2 scoring 0.98. In the subtype category, Site 2 achieved perfect scores, outperforming Site 1. This method enhances extraction accuracy and broadens its use across dental contexts. The study underscores AI and NLP’s transformative impact on healthcare delivery and management. Integrating AI and NLP technologies enhances documentation and simplifies administrative tasks by precisely extracting complex clinical information. This approach effectively addresses challenges in dental diagnostics. Using synthetic training data from LLMs optimizes the training process, improving accuracy and efficiency in identifying periodontal diagnoses from clinical notes. This innovative method holds promise for broader healthcare applications, potentially improving patient care quality.
摘要：本研究通过从非结构化文本中提取诊断信息来解决牙科记录中缺少结构化数据的问题。更新的牙周学分类系统的复杂性增加了不完整或遗漏的结构化诊断。为了解决这个问题，我们使用了先进的人工智能和NLP方法，利用GPT-4生成合成音符，用于微调Roberta模型。这大大增强了模型理解医学和牙科语言的能力。我们使用从两个数据集中随机选择的120个临床病历对该模型进行了评估，证明其提高了诊断提取的准确性。结果显示，诊断牙周状态、分期和分级的准确率很高，站点1得分为0.99，站点2得分为0.98。在亚型分类中，站点2获得了满分，表现优于站点1。这种方法提高了提取的准确性，并扩大了其在牙科环境中的应用。这项研究强调了人工智能和NLP对医疗保健提供和管理的变革性影响。集成AI和NLP技术通过精确提取复杂的临床信息来增强文档并简化管理任务。这种方法有效地解决了牙科诊断中的挑战。使用来自LLMS的合成训练数据优化了训练过程，提高了从临床记录中识别牙周诊断的准确性和效率。这种创新的方法有望在更广泛的医疗保健应用中发挥作用，潜在地提高患者的护理质量。

[NLP-59] Evaluating Long Range Dependency Handling in Code Generation Models using Multi-Step Key Retrieval
[NLP-59] 使用多步密钥检索评估代码生成模型中的长期依赖性处理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21049
作者: Yannick Assogba,Donghao Ren
关键词-EN: models support larger, language models support, larger context sizes, increasingly important, support larger
关键词-ZN: 模型支持更大，语言模型支持，更大的上下文大小，越来越重要，支持更大
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: 29 pages, 18 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:As language models support larger and larger context sizes, evaluating their ability to make effective use of that context becomes increasingly important. We analyze the ability of several code generation models to handle long range dependencies using a suite of multi-step key retrieval tasks in context windows up to 8k tokens in length. The tasks progressively increase in difficulty and allow more nuanced evaluation of model capabilities than tests like the popular needle-in-the-haystack test. We find that performance degrades significantly (up to 2x) when a function references another function that is defined later in the prompt. We also observe that models that use sliding window attention mechanisms have difficulty handling references further than the size of a single window. We perform simple prompt modifications using call graph information to improve multi-step retrieval performance up to 3x. Our analysis highlights different facets of long-context performance and is suggestive of prompt construction strategies for code completion tools
摘要：随着语言模型支持越来越大的语境，评估它们有效利用语境的能力变得越来越重要。我们分析了几种代码生成模型处理长依赖关系的能力，使用一套长达8k令牌的上下文窗口中的多步关键字检索任务。与流行的大海捞针测试相比，这些任务的难度逐渐增加，并允许对模型能力进行更细微的评估。我们发现，当一个函数引用在提示符后面定义的另一个函数时，性能会显著降低(高达2倍)。我们还观察到，使用滑动窗口注意机制的模型很难处理超出单个窗口大小的引用。我们使用调用图信息执行简单的提示修改，将多步检索性能提高高达3倍。我们的分析突出了长上下文性能的不同方面，并建议了代码完成工具的快速构建策略

[NLP-60] APTNESS: Incorporating Appraisal Theory and Emotion Support Strategies for Empathetic Response Generation CIKM2024
[NLP-60] APTNESS：用于产生同理心反应的综合评估理论和情感支持策略

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21048
作者: Yuxuan Hu,Minghuan Tan,Chenwei Zhang,Zixuan Li,Xiaodan Liang,Min Yang,Chengming Li,Xiping Hu
关键词-EN: resolving emotional challenges, generation is designed, designed to comprehend, comprehend the emotions, Empathetic response generation
关键词-ZN: 解决情感挑战，一代是设计的，旨在理解、理解情感，同理心反应的一代
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注: Appectped to CIKM2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Empathetic response generation is designed to comprehend the emotions of others and select the most appropriate strategies to assist them in resolving emotional challenges. Empathy can be categorized into cognitive empathy and affective empathy. The former pertains to the ability to understand and discern the emotional issues and situations of others, while the latter involves the capacity to provide comfort. To enhance one’s empathetic abilities, it is essential to develop both these aspects. Therefore, we develop an innovative framework that combines retrieval augmentation and emotional support strategy integration. Our framework starts with the introduction of a comprehensive emotional palette for empathy. We then apply appraisal theory to decompose this palette and create a database of empathetic responses. This database serves as an external resource and enhances the LLM’s empathy by integrating semantic retrieval mechanisms. Moreover, our framework places a strong emphasis on the proper articulation of response strategies. By incorporating emotional support strategies, we aim to enrich the model’s capabilities in both cognitive and affective empathy, leading to a more nuanced and comprehensive empathetic response. Finally, we extract datasets ED and ET from the empathetic dialogue dataset \textscEmpatheticDialogues and ExTES based on dialogue length. Experiments demonstrate that our framework can enhance the empathy ability of LLMs from both cognitive and affective empathy perspectives. Our code is released at this https URL.
摘要：移情反应的产生旨在理解他人的情绪，并选择最合适的策略来帮助他们解决情绪挑战。移情可分为认知移情和情感移情。前者涉及理解和辨别他人情绪问题和情况的能力，而后者涉及提供安慰的能力。要提高一个人的移情能力，这两个方面都是必不可少的。因此，我们开发了一个结合检索增强和情绪支持策略整合的创新框架。我们的框架首先引入了一个全面的情感调色板来表达同理心。然后，我们应用评估理论来分解这个调色板，并创建一个同理心反应的数据库。这个数据库作为外部资源，通过整合语义检索机制来增强LLM的同理心。此外，我们的框架非常强调适当地阐述应对战略。通过纳入情感支持策略，我们的目标是丰富模型在认知和情感移情方面的能力，导致更细微和全面的移情反应。最后，从移情对话数据集TextscEmpatheticDialog和ExTES中提取基于对话长度的数据集ED和Et。实验表明，我们的框架可以从认知和情感两个角度提高低收入者的共情能力。我们的代码在这个HTTPS URL上发布。

[NLP-61] Promises and Pitfalls of Generative Masked Language Modeling: Theoretical Framework and Practical Guidelines ICML2024
[NLP-61] 生成性掩蔽语言建模的承诺和陷阱：理论框架和实践指南

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21046
作者: Yuchen Li,Alexandre Kirchmeyer,Aashay Mehta,Yilong Qin,Boris Dadachev,Kishore Papineni,Sanjiv Kumar,Andrej Risteski
关键词-EN: Generative Masked Language, text generation, unidirectional generation, Masked Language Models, Autoregressive language models
关键词-ZN: 生成式掩蔽语言、文本生成、单向生成、掩蔽语言模型、自回归语言模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: ICML 2024

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Autoregressive language models are the currently dominant paradigm for text generation, but they have some fundamental limitations that cannot be remedied by scale-for example inherently sequential and unidirectional generation. While alternate classes of models have been explored, we have limited mathematical understanding of their fundamental power and limitations. In this paper we focus on Generative Masked Language Models (GMLMs), a non-autoregressive paradigm in which we train a model to fit conditional probabilities of the data distribution via masking, which are subsequently used as inputs to a Markov Chain to draw samples from the model, These models empirically strike a promising speed-quality trade-off as each step can be typically parallelized by decoding the entire sequence in parallel. We develop a mathematical framework for analyzing and improving such models which sheds light on questions of sample complexity and inference speed and quality. Empirically, we adapt the T5 model for iteratively-refined parallel decoding, achieving 2-3x speedup in machine translation with minimal sacrifice in quality compared with autoregressive models. We run careful ablation experiments to give recommendations on key design choices, and make fine-grained observations on the common error modes in connection with our theory. Our mathematical analyses and empirical observations characterize both potentials and limitations of this approach, and can be applied to future works on improving understanding and performance of GMLMs. Our codes are released at this https URL
摘要：自回归语言模型是当前文本生成的主流范式，但它们有一些基本的局限性，不能通过规模来弥补-例如内在的顺序和单向生成。虽然已经探索了不同类别的模型，但我们对它们的基本力量和局限性的数学理解有限。在本文中，我们关注生成掩蔽语言模型(GMLM)，这是一种非自回归范式，在该范例中，我们通过掩蔽来训练模型以适应数据分布的条件概率，然后将其用作马尔可夫链的输入以从模型中提取样本。这些模型在经验上达到了有希望的速度和质量之间的权衡，因为每一步都可以通过并行解码整个序列来典型地并行化。我们开发了一个数学框架来分析和改进这样的模型，它揭示了样本复杂性以及推理速度和质量的问题。在实验中，我们采用T5模型进行迭代精化并行译码，与自回归模型相比，机器翻译在质量上的损失最小，实现了2-3倍的加速。我们进行了仔细的烧蚀实验，以给出关键设计选择的建议，并根据我们的理论对常见的误差模式进行了细粒度的观察。我们的数学分析和经验观察表征了这种方法的潜力和局限性，并可以应用于未来提高对GMLM的理解和性能的工作。我们的代码在此HTTPS URL上发布

[NLP-62] Unlocking the Potential: Benchmarking Large Language Models in Water Engineering and Research
[NLP-62] 释放潜力：水工程和研究中的大型语言模型基准

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21045
作者: Boyan Xu,Liang Wen,Zihao Li,Yuxing Yang,Guanlan Wu,Xiongpeng Tang,Yu Li,Zihao Wu,Qingxian Su,Xueqing Shi,Yue Yang,Rui Tong,How Yong Ng
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, Large Language, water engineering, Language Models, water
关键词-ZN: 大型语言模型，大型语言，水工程，语言模型，水
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have sparked interest in their potential applications across various fields. This paper embarked on a pivotal inquiry: Can existing LLMs effectively serve as “water expert models” for water engineering and research tasks? This study was the first to evaluate LLMs’ contributions across various water engineering and research tasks by establishing a domain-specific benchmark suite, namely, WaterER. Herein, we prepared 983 tasks related to water engineering and research, categorized into “wastewater treatment”, “environmental restoration”, “drinking water treatment and distribution”, “sanitation”, “anaerobic digestion” and “contaminants assessment”. We evaluated the performance of seven LLMs (i.e., GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Gemini, GLM-4, ERNIE, QWEN and Llama3) on these tasks. We highlighted the strengths of GPT-4 in handling diverse and complex tasks of water engineering and water research, the specialized capabilities of Gemini in academic contexts, Llama3’s strongest capacity to answer Chinese water engineering questions and the competitive performance of Chinese-oriented models like GLM-4, ERNIE and QWEN in some water engineering tasks. More specifically, current LLMs excelled particularly in generating precise research gaps for papers on “contaminants and related water quality monitoring and assessment”. Additionally, they were more adept at creating appropriate titles for research papers on “treatment processes for wastewaters”, “environmental restoration”, and “drinking water treatment”. Overall, this study pioneered evaluating LLMs in water engineering and research by introducing the WaterER benchmark to assess the trustworthiness of their predictions. This standardized evaluation framework would also drive future advancements in LLM technology by using targeting datasets, propelling these models towards becoming true “water expert”.
摘要：大型语言模型的最新进展引起了人们对其在各个领域的潜在应用的兴趣。本文提出了一个关键问题：现有的LLM能否有效地作为水工程和研究任务的“水专家模型”？这项研究通过建立一个特定领域的基准套件，即Water，首次评估了LLMS在各种水工程和研究任务中的贡献。在这里，我们准备了983项与水工程和研究有关的任务，分为“废水处理”、“环境恢复”、“饮用水处理和分配”、“卫生”、“厌氧消化”和“污染物评估”。我们评估了7种LLMS(即GPT-4、GPT-3.5、Gemini、GLm-4、Ernie、QWEN和Llama3)在这些任务上的性能。我们强调了GPT-4在处理复杂多样的水利工程和水研究任务方面的优势，双子座在学术背景下的专业能力，Llama3的S回答中国水利工程问题的最强能力，以及GLm-4、Ernie和Qwen等面向中国的模型在一些水利工程任务中的竞争表现。更具体地说，当前的LLMS在为“污染物和相关水质监测和评估”的论文产生精确的研究空白方面尤其出色。此外，他们更善于为关于“废水处理过程”、“环境恢复”和“饮用水处理”的研究论文创造适当的标题。总体而言，这项研究率先在水工程和研究中评估LLMS，引入了Water基准来评估其预测的可信度。这一标准化的评估框架还将通过使用目标数据集来推动LLM技术的未来进步，推动这些模型成为真正的“水专家”。

[NLP-63] CP-Prompt: Composition-Based Cross-modal Prompting for Domain-Incremental Continual Learning
[NLP-63] CP-Promise：基于组合的跨模式预算分配，用于领域增量连续学习

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21043
作者: Yu Feng,Zhen Tian,Yifan Zhu,Zongfu Han,Haoran Luo,Guangwei Zhang,Meina Song
关键词-EN: cross-modal domain-incremental learning, domain-incremental learning, key challenge, challenge of cross-modal, cross-modal domain-incremental
关键词-ZN: 跨模式领域增量学习，领域增量学习，关键挑战，跨模式的挑战，跨模式领域增量
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:The key challenge of cross-modal domain-incremental learning (DIL) is to enable the learning model to continuously learn from novel data with different feature distributions under the same task without forgetting old ones. However, existing top-performing methods still cause high forgetting rates, by lacking intra-domain knowledge extraction and inter-domain common prompting strategy. In this paper, we propose a simple yet effective framework, CP-Prompt, by training limited parameters to instruct a pre-trained model to learn new domains and avoid forgetting existing feature distributions. CP-Prompt captures intra-domain knowledge by compositionally inserting personalized prompts on multi-head self-attention layers and then learns the inter-domain knowledge with a common prompting strategy. CP-Prompt shows superiority compared with state-of-the-art baselines among three widely evaluated DIL tasks. The source code is available at this https URL.
摘要：跨模式领域增量学习（DIL）的关键挑战是使学习模型能够在同一任务下不断从具有不同特征分布的新数据中学习，而不会忘记旧的数据。然而，现有的最佳性能方法由于缺乏领域内知识提取和领域间通用提示策略，仍然会导致较高的遗忘率。在本文中，我们提出了一个简单而有效的框架CP-Prompt，通过训练有限的参数来指示预训练的模型学习新的领域并避免忘记现有的特征分布。CP-Promise通过在多头自我关注层上组合插入个性化提示来捕获域内知识，然后通过通用提示策略学习域间知识。在三项广泛评估的DIL任务中，CP-Promise显示出与最先进的基线相比的优越性。源代码可在httpsURL中获取。

[NLP-64] hey Look Like Each Other: Case-based Reasoning for Explainable Depression Detection on Twitter using Large Language Models
[NLP-64] 嘿，看起来很像：使用大型语言模型在Twitter上进行可解释抑郁症检测的基于案例的推理

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21041
作者: Mohammad Saeid Mahdavinejad,Peyman Adibi,Amirhassan Monadjemi,Pascal Hitzler
关键词-EN: requires prompt diagnosis, diagnosis and treatment, issue that requires, requires prompt, prompt diagnosis
关键词-ZN: 需要及时诊断、诊断和治疗，需要及时、及时的诊断的问题
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Social and Information Networks (cs.SI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Depression is a common mental health issue that requires prompt diagnosis and treatment. Despite the promise of social media data for depression detection, the opacity of employed deep learning models hinders interpretability and raises bias concerns. We address this challenge by introducing ProtoDep, a novel, explainable framework for Twitter-based depression detection. ProtoDep leverages prototype learning and the generative power of Large Language Models to provide transparent explanations at three levels: (i) symptom-level explanations for each tweet and user, (ii) case-based explanations comparing the user to similar individuals, and (iii) transparent decision-making through classification weights. Evaluated on five benchmark datasets, ProtoDep achieves near state-of-the-art performance while learning meaningful prototypes. This multi-faceted approach offers significant potential to enhance the reliability and transparency of depression detection on social media, ultimately aiding mental health professionals in delivering more informed care.
摘要：抑郁症是一种常见的心理健康问题，需要及时诊断和治疗。尽管社交媒体数据有望用于抑郁症检测，但所采用的深度学习模型的不透明性阻碍了可解释性，并引发了人们对偏见的担忧。我们通过引入ProtoDep来应对这一挑战，ProtoDep是一个新颖的、可解释的框架，用于基于Twitter的抑郁症检测。ProtoDep利用原型学习和大型语言模型的生成能力，在三个层面上提供透明的解释：(I)针对每个推文和用户的症状级别解释，(Ii)基于案例的解释，将用户与相似的个人进行比较，以及(Iii)通过分类权重进行透明的决策。在五个基准数据集上进行评估，ProtoDep在学习有意义的原型的同时实现了接近最先进的性能。这种多方面的方法提供了巨大的潜力，可以提高社交媒体上抑郁症检测的可靠性和透明度，最终帮助精神卫生专业人员提供更知情的护理。

[NLP-65] owards Automated Data Sciences with Natural Language and SageCopilot: Practices and Lessons Learned
[NLP-65] owards使用自然语言和SageCopilot的自动数据科学：实践和经验教训

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21040
作者: Yuan Liao,Jiang Bian,Yuhui Yun,Shuo Wang,Yubo Zhang,Jiaming Chu,Tao Wang,Kewei Li,Yuchen Li,Xuhong Li,Shilei Ji,Haoyi Xiong
关键词-EN: encompassing data querying, achieving full automation, translating natural language, natural language instructions, made significant advancements
关键词-ZN: 涵盖数据查询、实现完全自动化、翻译自然语言、自然语言指令，取得了重大进展
类目: Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Databases (cs.DB); Software Engineering (cs.SE)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:While the field of NL2SQL has made significant advancements in translating natural language instructions into executable SQL scripts for data querying and processing, achieving full automation within the broader data science pipeline - encompassing data querying, analysis, visualization, and reporting - remains a complex challenge. This study introduces SageCopilot, an advanced, industry-grade system system that automates the data science pipeline by integrating Large Language Models (LLMs), Autonomous Agents (AutoAgents), and Language User Interfaces (LUIs). Specifically, SageCopilot incorporates a two-phase design: an online component refining users’ inputs into executable scripts through In-Context Learning (ICL) and running the scripts for results reporting visualization, and an offline preparing demonstrations requested by ICL in the online phase. A list of trending strategies such as Chain-of-Thought and prompt-tuning have been used to augment SageCopilot for enhanced performance. Through rigorous testing and comparative analysis against prompt-based solutions, SageCopilot has been empirically validated to achieve superior end-to-end performance in generating or executing scripts and offering results with visualization, backed by real-world datasets. Our in-depth ablation studies highlight the individual contributions of various components and strategies used by SageCopilot to the end-to-end correctness for data sciences.
摘要：尽管NL2SQL领域在将自然语言指令转换为用于数据查询和处理的可执行SQL脚本方面取得了重大进展，但在更广泛的数据科学管道内实现完全自动化-包括数据查询、分析、可视化和报告-仍然是一个复杂的挑战。这项研究介绍了SageCopilot，这是一个先进的工业级系统系统，通过集成大型语言模型(LLM)、自治代理(AutoAgents)和语言用户界面(LUIS)来自动化数据科学管道。具体地说，SageCopilot结合了两个阶段的设计：在线组件通过上下文中学习(ICL)将用户的输入提炼成可执行脚本并运行脚本以实现结果报告可视化，以及在线阶段根据ICL要求的离线准备演示。一系列流行的策略，如思考链和即时调整，已经被用来增强SageCopilot的性能。通过对基于提示符的解决方案进行严格的测试和比较分析，SageCopilot经过了经验验证，在生成或执行脚本以及在真实数据集支持下以可视化方式提供结果方面实现了卓越的端到端性能。我们深入的消融研究突出了SageCopilot使用的各种组件和策略对数据科学端到端正确性的个别贡献。

[NLP-66] Mapping Patient Trajectories: Understanding and Visualizing Sepsis Prognostic Pathways from Patients Clinical Narratives
[NLP-66] 绘制患者轨迹：从患者临床叙述中了解和可视化脓毒症预后途径

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21039
作者: Sudeshna Jana,Tirthankar Dasgupta,Lipika Dey
关键词-EN: Electronic Health Records, evidence-based patient care, Natural Language Processing, recent years, professionals are increasingly
关键词-ZN: 电子健康记录、循证患者护理、自然语言处理，近年来，专业人士越来越多
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Machine Learning (cs.LG)
备注: preprint, 8 pages, 6 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In recent years, healthcare professionals are increasingly emphasizing on personalized and evidence-based patient care through the exploration of prognostic pathways. To study this, structured clinical variables from Electronic Health Records (EHRs) data have traditionally been employed by many researchers. Presently, Natural Language Processing models have received great attention in clinical research which expanded the possibilities of using clinical narratives. In this paper, we propose a systematic methodology for developing sepsis prognostic pathways derived from clinical notes, focusing on diverse patient subgroups identified by exploring comorbidities associated with sepsis and generating explanations of these subgroups using SHAP. The extracted prognostic pathways of these subgroups provide valuable insights into the dynamic trajectories of sepsis severity over time. Visualizing these pathways sheds light on the likelihood and direction of disease progression across various contexts and reveals patterns and pivotal factors or biomarkers influencing the transition between sepsis stages, whether toward deterioration or improvement. This empowers healthcare providers to implement more personalized and effective healthcare strategies for individual patients.
摘要：近年来，通过探索预后路径，医护人员越来越重视个性化和循证的病人护理。为了研究这一点，许多研究人员传统上使用来自电子健康记录(EHR)数据的结构化临床变量。目前，自然语言处理模型在临床研究中受到了极大的关注，这扩大了临床叙述使用的可能性。在这篇文章中，我们提出了一种系统的方法来开发来自临床记录的脓毒症预后路径，重点是通过探索与脓毒症相关的共病并使用Shap对这些亚组进行解释来识别不同的患者亚组。提取的这些亚组的预后路径为了解脓毒症严重程度随时间的动态轨迹提供了有价值的见解。可视化这些途径有助于了解各种情况下疾病进展的可能性和方向，并揭示影响脓毒症阶段之间过渡的模式和关键因素或生物标记物，无论是恶化还是改善。这使医疗保健提供者能够为个别患者实施更个性化、更有效的医疗保健策略。

[NLP-67] Advancing Chart Question Answering with Robust Chart Component Recognition
[NLP-67] 利用稳健的图表组件识别推进图表问题解答

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21038
作者: Hanwen Zheng,Sijia Wang,Chris Thomas,Lifu Huang
关键词-EN: comprehension presents significant, presents significant challenges, Chart comprehension presents, machine learning models, learning models due
关键词-ZN: 理解提出重大挑战，图表理解提出，机器学习模型，学习模型
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Information Retrieval (cs.IR)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Chart comprehension presents significant challenges for machine learning models due to the diverse and intricate shapes of charts. Existing multimodal methods often overlook these visual features or fail to integrate them effectively for chart question answering (ChartQA). To address this, we introduce Chartformer, a unified framework that enhances chart component recognition by accurately identifying and classifying components such as bars, lines, pies, titles, legends, and axes. Additionally, we propose a novel Question-guided Deformable Co-Attention (QDCAt) mechanism, which fuses chart features encoded by Chartformer with the given question, leveraging the question’s guidance to ground the correct answer. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed approaches significantly outperform baseline models in chart component recognition and ChartQA tasks, achieving improvements of 3.2% in mAP and 15.4% in accuracy, respectively. These results underscore the robustness of our solution for detailed visual data interpretation across various applications.
摘要：由于图表形状的多样性和复杂性，图表的理解对机器学习模型提出了巨大的挑战。现有的多通道方法往往忽略了这些视觉特征，或者不能有效地将它们整合到图表问答中。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了Chartformer，这是一个统一的框架，它通过准确地识别和分类条形图、线条、饼图、标题、图例和轴等组件来增强图表组件识别。此外，我们还提出了一种新颖的问题引导可变形协同注意(QDCAt)机制，该机制将Chartform编码的图表特征与给定的问题融合在一起，利用问题的指导来找到正确的答案。大量实验表明，该方法在海图组件识别和ChartQA任务中的性能明显优于基线模型，在MAP和ChartQA任务中的准确率分别提高了3.2%和15.4%。这些结果突显了我们的解决方案在各种应用程序中用于详细的视觉数据解释的健壮性。

[NLP-68] An Application of Large Language Models to Coding Negotiation Transcripts
[NLP-68] 大型语言模型在谈判成绩单编码中的应用

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21037
作者: Ray Friedman,Jaewoo Cho,Jeanne Brett,Xuhui Zhan,Ningyu Han,Sriram Kannan,Yingxiang Ma,Jesse Spencer-Smith,Elisabeth Jäckel,Alfred Zerres,Madison Hooper,Katie Babbit,Manish Acharya,Wendi Adair,Soroush Aslani,Tayfun Aykaç,Chris Bauman,Rebecca Bennett,Garrett Brady,Peggy Briggs,Cheryl Dowie,Chase Eck,Igmar Geiger,Frank Jacob,Molly Kern,Sujin Lee,Leigh Anne Liu,Wu Liu,Jeffrey Loewenstein,Anne Lytle,Li Ma,Michel Mann,Alexandra Mislin,Tyree Mitchell,Hannah Martensen née Nagler,Amit Nandkeolyar,Mara Olekalns,Elena Paliakova,Jennifer Parlamis,Jason Pierce,Nancy Pierce,Robin Pinkley,Nathalie Prime,Jimena Ramirez-Marin,Kevin Rockmann,William Ross,Zhaleh Semnani-Azad,Juliana Schroeder,Philip Smith,Elena Stimmer,Roderick Swaab,Leigh Thompson,Cathy Tinsley,Ece Tuncel,Laurie Weingart,Robert Wilken,JingJing Yao,Zhi-Xue Zhang
关键词-EN: Large Language Models, natural language processing, Large Language, demonstrated impressive capabilities, language processing
关键词-ZN: 大型语言模型、自然语言处理、大型语言、展示了令人印象深刻的能力、语言处理
类目: Computation and Language (cs.CL); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:In recent years, Large Language Models (LLM) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in the field of natural language processing (NLP). This paper explores the application of LLMs in negotiation transcript analysis by the Vanderbilt AI Negotiation Lab. Starting in September 2022, we applied multiple strategies using LLMs from zero shot learning to fine tuning models to in-context learning). The final strategy we developed is explained, along with how to access and use the model. This study provides a sense of both the opportunities and roadblocks for the implementation of LLMs in real life applications and offers a model for how LLMs can be applied to coding in other fields.
摘要：近年来，大型语言模型（LLM）在自然语言处理（NLP）领域展示了令人印象深刻的能力。本文探讨了范德比尔特人工智能谈判实验室在谈判笔录分析中的LLM应用。从2022年9月开始，我们使用LLM应用了多种策略，从零学习到微调模型，再到上下文学习）。解释了我们开发的最终策略，以及如何访问和使用该模型。这项研究揭示了在现实生活中实施LLM的机会和障碍，并为LLM如何应用于其他领域的编码提供了一个模型。

[NLP-69] Multi-Grained Query-Guided Set Prediction Network for Grounded Multimodal Named Entity Recognition
[NLP-69] 用于接地多模式命名实体识别的多粒度查询引导集预测网络

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21033
作者: Jielong Tang,Zhenxing Wang,Ziyang Gong,Jianxing Yu,Shuang Wang,Jian Yin
关键词-EN: Named Entity Recognition, simultaneously extract entity, Entity Recognition, Grounded Multimodal Named, emerging information extraction
关键词-ZN: 命名实体识别、同时提取实体、实体识别、接地多模式命名、新兴信息提取
类目: Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)
备注: 11 pages, 5 figures

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Grounded Multimodal Named Entity Recognition (GMNER) is an emerging information extraction (IE) task, aiming to simultaneously extract entity spans, types, and entity-matched bounding box groundings in images from given sentence-image pairs data. Recent unified methods employing machine reading comprehension (MRC-based) frameworks or sequence generation-based models face challenges in understanding the relationships of multimodal entities. MRC-based frameworks, utilizing human-designed queries, struggle to model intra-entity connections. Meanwhile, sequence generation-based outputs excessively rely on inter-entity dependencies due to pre-defined decoding order. To tackle these, we propose a novel unified framework named Multi-grained Query-guided Set Prediction Network (MQSPN) to learn appropriate relationships at intra-entity and inter-entity levels. Specifically, MQSPN consists of a Multi-grained Query Set (MQS) and a Multimodal Set Prediction Network (MSP). MQS combines specific type-grained and learnable entity-grained queries to adaptively strengthen intra-entity connections by explicitly aligning visual regions with textual spans. Based on solid intra-entity modeling, MSP reformulates GMNER as a set prediction, enabling the parallel prediction of multimodal entities in a non-autoregressive manner, eliminating redundant dependencies from preceding sequences, and guiding models to establish appropriate inter-entity relationships from a global matching perspective. Additionally, to boost better alignment of two-level relationships, we also incorporate a Query-guided Fusion Net (QFNet) to work as a glue network between MQS and MSP. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performances in widely used benchmarks. Notably, our method improves 2.83% F1 in the difficult fine-grained GMNER benchmark.
摘要：接地多模式命名实体识别(GMNER)是一项新兴的信息提取(IE)任务，旨在从给定的句子图像对数据中同时提取图像中的实体跨度、类型和实体匹配的边界框定位。最近使用机器阅读理解(MRC)框架或基于序列生成的模型的统一方法在理解多通道实体的关系方面面临挑战。基于MRC的框架，利用人工设计的查询，很难对实体内的连接进行建模。同时，由于预定义的解码顺序，基于序列生成的输出过度依赖于实体间的依赖关系。针对这些问题，我们提出了一种新的统一框架，称为多粒度查询引导集预测网络(MQSPN)，用于在实体内部和实体间学习合适的关系。具体来说，MQSPN由一个多粒度查询集(MQS)和一个多模集预测网络(MSP)组成。MQS结合了特定的类型粒度和可学习的实体粒度查询，通过显式地将可视区域与文本范围对齐来自适应地加强实体内的连接。基于实体内实体建模，MSP将GMNER重新表述为集合预测，以非自回归的方式实现多通道实体的并行预测，消除先前序列中的冗余依赖，并从全局匹配的角度指导模型建立适当的实体间关系。此外，为了更好地协调两级关系，我们还引入了一个查询引导的融合网(QFNet)来作为MQS和MSP之间的粘合网络。大量的实验表明，我们的方法在广泛使用的基准测试中取得了最好的性能。值得注意的是，我们的方法在困难的细粒度GMNER基准中提高了2.83%的F1。

[NLP-70] LLM-Find: An Autonomous GIS Agent Framework for Geospatial Data Retrieval
[NLP-70] LLLM-Find：一种用于地理空间数据检索的自治地理信息系统代理框架

链接: https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21024
作者: Huan Ning,Zhenlong Li,Temitope Akinboyewa,M. Naser Lessani
关键词-EN: large language models, geographic information systems, emerging large language, accomplish spatial analyses, autonomous geographic information
关键词-ZN: 大型语言模型、地理信息系统、新兴大型语言、完成空间分析、自治地理信息
类目: Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Emerging Technologies (cs.ET)
备注:

点击查看摘要

Abstract:Powered by the emerging large language models (LLMs), autonomous geographic information systems (GIS) agents have the potential to accomplish spatial analyses and cartographic tasks. However, a research gap exists to support fully autonomous GIS agents: how to enable agents to discover and download the necessary data for geospatial analyses. This study proposes LLM-Find, an autonomous GIS agent framework capable of selecting and fetching required geospatial data by generating, executing, and debugging programs. LLM-Find utilizes the LLM as the decision-maker, selects the appropriate data source (s) from a pre-defined source list, and fetches the data from the chosen source. Each data source has a handbook that records the metadata and technical details for data retrieval. The proposed framework is designed in a plug-and-play style to ensure flexibility and extensibility. Human users or autonomous data scrawlers can add a new data source by adding a new handbook. We developed a prototype agent based on LLM-Find, and experiment results demonstrate its capability of retrieving data from various sources including OpenStreetMap, administrative boundaries and demographic data from the US Census Bureau, satellite basemaps from ESRI World Imagery, weather data from a commercial provider, and the COVID-19 data from the NYTimes GitHub. Our study is among the first attempts to develop an autonomous geospatial data retrieval agent.
